what_it_does: This file defines a full-page React screen (`ProjectsScreen`) for browsing all projects, plus a `ProjectCard` component. It renders a filterable, sortable grid of project cards using global `window.MS_DATA`, with search, filter tabs, sort controls, and a call-to-action to start a new project.
observations: Uses React hooks aliased as `useStateP`, `useEffectP`, and `useMemoP`
Reads project data from `window.MS_DATA` and reports context via `window.ViContext`
Implements filter tabs: all, active, shaping, review, package
Implements sort modes: recent, progress, name
Implements a search input filtering by title and subtitle
`recencyRank` parses the human-readable `last_activity` string to produce a numeric rank
`phaseBucket` maps individual phases into coarser UI buckets: shaping, review, package, live
The progress track fill width is based on phase index, while the percentage text uses `project.progress`
Includes an empty state with a clear-filters action
`ProjectCard` is a `<button>` containing block-level elements and an image
standouts: Recency is inferred by substring matching on `last_activity` strings
Phase progress bar is driven by `phaseIndex / (totalPhases - 1)`, not by `project.progress`
The `live` bucket exists in `phaseBucket` but has no corresponding filter tab
conventions: Functional React components with destructured props
Global `window` singletons for data and context
concerns: Progress bar width and displayed percentage can disagree because they use different data sources
`recencyRank` is brittle: it depends on exact English substrings in `last_activity`
`phaseBucket` returns `live` for unknown phases, but no UI filter exposes that bucket
No guards if `window.MS_DATA` is missing or malformed
`<button>` contains block-level and image content, which can cause invalid HTML and accessibility issues
No `key` prop issues, but no ARIA labels or focus management either
notes: A straightforward project dashboard screen that couples tightly to global window data and a phase-bucket taxonomy.
questions: What guarantees does `window.MS_DATA` provide about shape and mutability?
Why are React hooks aliased with a `P` suffix?
Why does the progress bar use phase index rather than `project.progress`?
Where are the styles for the BEM classes defined?
Is `Icon` globally registered, and what icon names are valid?
What does the `active` boolean on a project signify versus its `phase`?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a dedicated full-page project viewer inside the Studio shell; the file matches that purpose.
intention_signals: Provide a calm, scannable home base where users can find any project, see its current phase and progress, and easily start something new.
novelty: The combination of fuzzy string-based recency ranking and phase-index-driven progress visualization is somewhat unusual; otherwise it is a conventional project grid.
contribution: A reusable project-listing screen and card component that a fused system could keep for browsing, filtering, sorting, and opening projects, provided the global data contract and phase taxonomy are preserved.
summary_for_embedding: A React screen component that renders a filterable, sortable grid of project cards from global window data, together with a companion card component showing cover, phase, progress, and activity metadata.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the main projects dashboard where users browse, search, and open existing projects or start a new one.

symbols.does: Maps a project's `last_activity` string to a numeric recency rank for sorting.
symbols.description: `recencyRank` inspects the lowercase `last_activity` string for substrings like 'just now', 'minute', 'hour', 'day', and 'week', returning smaller numbers for more recent activity. It is a brittle, string-based heuristic used only for the 'recent' sort order.

symbols.does: Maps a project phase string to a coarse UI bucket used by the filter tabs.
symbols.description: `phaseBucket` groups the fine-grained phases 'concept', 'direction', 'renderer', and 'sequence' into 'shaping'; 'generate', 'review', and 'extract' into 'review'; 'package' and 'handoff' into 'package'; and everything else into 'live'. This mapping decouples the internal phase model from the filter UI.

symbols.does: Renders a single project card with cover image, title, phase, progress, sequence count, and last activity.
symbols.description: `ProjectCard` is a button component that displays a project's cover, active badge, title, subtitle, current phase label with icon, progress percentage, a progress track filled according to phase index, and metadata showing sequence count and recency. It calls `onOpen` when clicked.
